data.body:
## Description

**What**: Add a one-time scrutiny opt-in question to /roadmap — if yes, invoke the shared scrutiny pipeline per-epic; if no, write a scrutiny:pending tag to each epic's ticket
**Why**: Practitioners using /roadmap should get the same scrutiny quality as /brainstorm, with an explicit opt-out path that leaves a machine-readable caveat for downstream skills
**Scope**:
- IN: One-time question in /roadmap flow; invoke shared pipeline per-epic when opted in; write scrutiny:pending tag when opted out; tag written via ticket system tag field
- OUT: Changing scrutiny pipeline behavior; default-deny logic for missing tags (Story 5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, /roadmap asks a one-time "apply full scrutiny?" question and respects the answer for all epics in the session
  ← Satisfies: "/roadmap asks a one-time apply full scrutiny to each epic? question"
- When this story is complete, opting out writes a scrutiny:pending tag to each epic ticket, readable via ticket show
  ← Satisfies: "each epic's ticket includes a structured scrutiny:pending tag readable by downstream skills"
- When this story is complete, the scrutiny:pending tag contract (field name, allowed values, semantics) is documented in a single location referenced by writer and readers
  ← Satisfies: "structured scrutiny:pending tag readable by downstream skills"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD DoD

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Per-epic scrutiny may hit tool timeout on large roadmaps — run scrutiny per-epic inline (not batched)
- [Maintainability] Tag contract must be defined once and referenced by both writer (/roadmap) and readers (/preplanning, /implementation-plan)
